metadata.seed: I was doing okay, but, I mean, I got comfortable with that extra income, and so now my fear is that I've gotten too comfortable with it, and now that it's not there anymore, it's going to affect the fact I'm not going to be able to pay for food or my rent.

patient.query: I was doing okay, but, I mean, I got comfortable with that extra income, and so now my fear is that I've gotten too comfortable with it, and now that it's not there anymore, it's going to affect the fact I'm not going to be able to pay for food or my rent.

automatic_thoughts: I won't be able to pay for food
I won't be able to pay my rent

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It makes sense that you're feeling worried about losing that extra income, especially since you've become accustomed to it. Can you tell me more about the specific thoughts that are contributing to this fear?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I've just been thinking about how tight things are already. Losing that extra income feels like it would tip me over the edge. I keep imagining all the bills piling up and what would happen if I can’t keep up. It’s like this constant weight on my chest.

automatic_thoughts: it would tip me over the edge
what would happen if I can’t keep up

llm_response.response: It sounds like the worry about financial strain is creating a heavy feeling for you. When you mentioned imagining bills piling up, I wonder if those thoughts feel overwhelming or if there might be other ways to look at this situation.

patient.query: It definitely feels overwhelming. I just keep picturing all the “what ifs” and it’s like I can’t breathe sometimes. It’s hard to see any other way right now. I want to believe I can handle it, but it all just feels so daunting and, I don’t know, hopeless at times.

automatic_thoughts: picturing all the 'what ifs'
I can't breathe sometimes
I can't handle it
it feels so daunting
it feels hopeless

neutral: Anxiety about not coping with parenthood (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like those thoughts are really weighing on you, and the physical sensations must be incredibly distressing. When you're feeling this way, it can be hard to imagine another way to look at things, but I wonder if there might be other ways to understand what's happening right now.

patient.query: It definitely feels overwhelming. I keep imagining all the "what ifs," and it’s hard to breathe sometimes. It's like this constant knot in my stomach. I tell myself I can handle it, but it just feels so heavy and hopeless at times.

automatic_thoughts: I keep imagining all the 'what ifs'
I can handle it
It feels so heavy and hopeless

llm_response.response: It sounds like those "what ifs" are creating a lot of pressure for you, and the physical sensations you're describing must be really uncomfortable. When you say you feel heavy and hopeless, can you tell me more about what's making those thoughts feel so persistent?

patient.query: I think it’s just this fear that I won’t be able to provide for myself or my family. Like, what if I can't pay the rent or buy groceries? I feel like I've always had to be the one who sorts things out, and not being able to do that feels like a failure. I guess it just keeps looping in my mind, you know?

triggers: fear of not providing for family
automatic_thoughts: I won't be able to provide for myself or my family
What if I can't pay the rent or buy groceries?
Not being able to sort things out feels like a failure

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of worry about not being able to provide for yourself and your family, and that's clearly weighing on you. Many people feel this kind of pressure, especially when they've always been the one to handle things, and it's completely normal to find it distressing.

patient.query: Yeah, it is really heavy. I just hate feeling like I'm not in control of things, like everything is slipping away. I mean, I know life can be unpredictable, but it just feels so personal right now, like I’m somehow failing. I don't want to let anyone down, especially not those who rely on me.

triggers: not wanting to let others down
automatic_thoughts: everything is slipping away
I'm somehow failing

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the pressure to always be in control is really weighing on you. When you mentioned everything feeling like it's slipping away, I wonder if you've ever considered that maybe not having all the answers could actually be okay? What would happen if someone else handled the situation the way you're struggling with right now?

patient.query: I don’t know... It just feels like if I let go of that control, everything might fall apart. I guess I just can't picture anyone else in my shoes, doing it the way I think it needs to be done. It's like, if I'm not the one steering the ship, then who will make sure it doesn't sink? That thought really scares me.

automatic_thoughts: if I let go of control, everything might fall apart
I can't picture anyone else doing it the way I think it needs to be done
if I'm not steering the ship, who will make sure it doesn't sink?

llm_response.response:
FINAL RESPONSE:
"It sounds like you're feeling a lot of pressure to maintain control, and that thought about being the only one who can steer feels really intense. When you say 'no one else could do it the right way,' what makes you think that's true?"

patient.query: I don't know, it just feels like I’ve always been the one who knows how things work, you know? Maybe it’s just me being hard on myself, but I worry that if someone else tries, they'll mess it up, and then everything will go wrong. I guess there’s this voice in my head that tells me I have to be perfect, that I have to meet everyone's expectations, and that if I don’t, it’ll all fall apart. It’s exhausting.

automatic_thoughts: I have to be perfect
I have to meet everyone's expectations
If I don’t, it’ll all fall apart
If someone else tries, they'll mess it up

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response:
FINAL RESPONSE:
"It sounds like you're carrying a heavy burden, feeling responsible for everything and afraid that if you don't step in, things will collapse. When you mentioned that voice telling you to be perfect, I wonder if that pressure is actually making it harder for others to succeed on their own? Sometimes we forget that people can handle things without us being 'the only one who knows.' "

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I see what you mean. It's like I’m holding onto this idea that I have to be the one who has it all figured out, but maybe that’s not realistic. I don’t want to push everyone away by trying to do it all myself, but it’s just so hard to let go of that. I feel like if I don't, I'm somehow letting everyone down, and that thought just keeps looping in my head.

automatic_thoughts: I have to be the one who has it all figured out
If I don't, I'm somehow letting everyone down
behaviors: trying to do it all myself

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It makes sense that this thought feels overwhelming. When you mentioned feeling like you're letting everyone down, what comes to mind when you imagine a situation where you're not the only one handling things?

patient.query: I guess I start to picture chaos, like everything just spiraling out of control. I think about the little things that could go wrong and suddenly they feel huge. It’s almost like I can’t trust anyone else to pick up the slack, and I get this sense of panic, like if I don’t do it, nothing will get done right. It’s tough to even imagine someone else in that role without feeling that knot in my stomach.

automatic_thoughts: everything just spiraling out of control
the little things that could go wrong feel huge
I can’t trust anyone else to pick up the slack
if I don’t do it, nothing will get done right
I can’t imagine someone else in that role

llm_response.response: It sounds like those thoughts about things spiraling out of control are really unsettling for you. Can you tell me more about how often these thoughts show up and what makes them feel more or less intense?
